__comment: Session-Token Lifecycle audit dashboard. HELD — import after the audit pipeline is fed (see docs/plans/session-token-audit-logs-plan.md). Replace ${DS_LOKI}/${DS_PG} with the real datasource UIDs at import. Panels correlate the three audit sources by jit_session_id / jti.
title: Session-Token Lifecycle (zero-trust audit)

panels.title: Approvals — who asked / who approved / scope (WORM jit_session)

targets.rawSql: SELECT created_at, id AS jit_session_id, requester_sub, approver_sub, scope_hash, state FROM jit_session WHERE ('$jit_session_id' = '' OR id = '$jit_session_id') ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 200;

panels.title: Tokens minted vs consumed vs denied (last 24h)

targets.rawSql: SELECT count(*) AS minted FROM jit_session WHERE state IN ('issued','consumed') AND created_at > now() - interval '24 hours';
SELECT count(*) AS consumed FROM consumed_jti WHERE consumed_at > now() - interval '24 hours';

panels.title: ext-proc — token USE for this session (grant_result / decision / tool)

targets.expr: {namespace="mcp-gateway", app="ext-proc-delegation"} | json | jit_session_id =~ "$jit_session_id.*" | line_format "{{.decision}} grant={{.grant_result}} injected={{.credential_injected}} tool={{.mcp_tool}} user={{.caller_username}} sid={{.jit_session_id}}"

panels.title: FAIL-OPEN CANARY — decision=allow with grant_result!=valid (MUST be empty)
panels.description: Any row here is a fail-open: the gate allowed a call whose grant did not validate. Should be impossible — alert on it.
